title: Guard relation persistence decision
goal: Decide whether persistent Guard Relation Cards should remain blocked or proceed, using the goldset false-positive/unresolved-case evidence, concrete consumer need and explicit precision/recall thresholds.

acceptance.assertion: The decision names a concrete consumer or records that no consumer justifies persistence.
Acceptable precision/recall and unresolved-case thresholds are recorded before persistence is allowed.
Keeping persistence blocked is an acceptable verified outcome if evidence does not justify promotion.

metadata.outcome: Implemented in Lenskit PR #922: persistent Guard Relation Cards remain blocked by explicit machine-readable decision. No concrete current consumer is established; current goldset precision is 0.666667, false-positive rate is 1.0, and unresolved cases remain present.
validation: Local before merge: 142 guard relation decision/goldset/audit/relation-card tests passed.

Post-merge on origin/main c465644d5ff1b49a221b11b000bcf201bd24ad18: same 142 tests passed, evaluator ran, JSON parsed, targeted ruff passed, planning-registration no new drift, doc-freshness PASS and diff-check pass.

verified_acceptance.rpu-v1-t009-consumer: No concrete current consumer justifies persistent Guard Relation Cards.
verified_acceptance.rpu-v1-t009-thresholds: Required thresholds recorded: named consumer, precision >=0.95, resolved-positive recall >=0.95, false-positive rate <=0.05, zero unresolved cases per relation type, preserved negative semantics.
verified_acceptance.rpu-v1-t009-block-ok: Persistence remains blocked because current evidence fails consumer, precision, false-positive-rate and unresolved-case criteria.
